purpose: PESS-style Dietary Exposure MCP demonstration using public source anchors and illustrative screening inputs.

sourceAnchors.title: Modeling external exposure to pesticides in human populations: Developing an exposure scenario generator
sourceAnchors.retrievalMethod: local_pdf_supplied_by_user; public DOI recorded for release package
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Comparison anchor for PESS dietary-module method, route integration, Monte Carlo framing, and biomonitoring plausibility context.
lockedFacts: PESS includes occupational, environmental, and dietary pesticide exposure modules.
The paper demonstrates endosulfan, mancozeb, and glyphosate.
The dietary module combines EFSA food consumption data with EFSA pesticide residue data.
PESS reports Monte Carlo dietary distributions and notes that negative normal-consumption draws are truncated to zero.

sourceAnchors.title: EFSA Food consumption data report page
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for EFSA Comprehensive European Food Consumption Database context.
lockedFacts: EFSA describes the Comprehensive Food Consumption Database as a source of food consumption information across the EU.
EFSA states the database supports exposure estimates for hazards in food.
EFSA reports chronic and acute consumption statistics by population and food categories.

sourceAnchors.title: EFSA Chemical monitoring data report page
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for pesticide-residue monitoring and annual-report context.
lockedFacts: EFSA describes chemical monitoring data collection as covering contaminants, food additives, pesticide residues, and veterinary medicinal product residues.
EFSA links pesticide residues in food to Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 and annual pesticide-residue reports.
The page exposes pesticide monitoring dashboards and annual-report links.

sourceAnchors.title: EU Pesticides Database
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for EU active-substance and MRL lookup context.
lockedFacts: The database supports searching active substances, food products, pesticide residues, and emergency authorisations.
The Commission states official information is published in the Official Journal of the European Union.
The page exposes data download and API routes for pesticide information.

sourceAnchors.title: EU legislation on pesticide maximum residue levels
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for MRL scope, default-MRL context, and consumer-safety framing.
lockedFacts: EU legislation harmonises pesticide MRLs for agricultural products for food or feed.
The page states MRLs apply to fresh products and processed products after dilution or concentration adjustment.
The page states a default MRL applies where a pesticide is not specifically mentioned.

sourceAnchors.title: EFSA Chemical Hazards Database - OpenFoodTox
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for toxicological reference-value infrastructure.
lockedFacts: OpenFoodTox provides structured hazard data from EFSA risk assessments.
The database covers pesticides, contaminants, food contact materials, additives, feed additives, and nutrients.
OpenFoodTox includes toxicological reference values and reference points.

sourceAnchors.title: EFSA glyphosate topic page
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for glyphosate regulatory context.
lockedFacts: EFSA describes glyphosate as a widely used herbicide.
The page states glyphosate is currently approved as an active substance in the EU until 15 December 2033.
The page distinguishes active-substance approval from national product authorisation.

screeningInputPosture.residueValues: Illustrative regulatory-screening inputs selected to exercise current Dietary MCP contracts; not row-level EFSA monitoring records.
screeningInputPosture.consumptionValues: Current governed Dietary MCP consumption profiles and a compact synthetic raw-survey fixture; not a full EFSA dashboard export.
screeningInputPosture.chemicalIdentity: Glyphosate selected because the current repo has the strongest defaults coverage for reference values, occurrence evidence, method evidence, MRL/trade records, and examples.

matrixSemantics.consumptionProxyRationale: This public slice uses the governed apples consumption amount as a compact apple-juice derivative fixture; it is not a row-level apple-juice survey estimate.

nonClaims: No EFSA PRIMo, DEEM, or PESS engine execution is claimed.
No official equivalence to EFSA monitoring datasets or dashboards is claimed.
No legal clearance, final regulatory decision, trade clearance, or PBPK internal-dose simulation is claimed.
